# Copilot instructions for TLibEv

TLibEv is an object-oriented, **line-by-line port of libev 4.33** to Delphi and
Free Pascal / Lazarus. The whole library is one unit, `src/LibEv.pas`; the
`examples/` folder holds self-testing demo programs. Apply the conventions below
when reviewing pull requests or proposing changes.

## Fidelity to libev
- Follow libev's C source closely. Every ported method carries an
  `{ ev.c:NNNN name }` (or `ev.h:NNNN`) comment pointing at its C counterpart —
  keep these accurate when code moves and add one for new ported logic.
- Prefer matching libev's algorithm and structure over "improving" it. Any
  deviation must be deliberate and documented (see the header of `LibEv.pas`
  and the deviation notes in `README.md` / `docs/MANUAL.md`).

## Cross-compiler and cross-platform
- The same source must compile on **Delphi** (`dcc32`/`dcc64`/`dcclinux64`) and
  **Free Pascal** (`{$MODE DELPHI}`). Guard compiler-specific code with
  `{$IFDEF FPC}`.
- Targets: **Linux** (x86-64, ARM64, 32-bit ARM), **macOS** (Apple Silicon) and
  **Windows**. Guard OS-specific code with `{$IFDEF LINUX}` / `{$IFDEF DARWIN}`
  / `{$IFDEF MSWINDOWS}`; `EV_POSIX` marks the shared POSIX targets (Linux or
  Darwin). Linux-only kernel interfaces (epoll, inotify, eventfd, signalfd,
  timerfd, child watchers) stay under a plain `LINUX` guard.
- On FPC, take platform types, structs, syscalls and the errno / flag /
  signal-number constants from the **RTL** (`BaseUnix`, `UnixType`, `Unix`,
  `Linux`, `Sockets`, `termio`) so they are correct per OS and architecture. On
  Delphi use `Posix.*` / `Winsock2`. Do **not** hand-declare a `cdecl external`
  when the RTL already provides it; only bind manually what the RTL genuinely
  lacks (e.g. `posix_openpt`, `_getwch`, the winsock `sendto` prototype), and
  explain why in a comment.
- Architecture-sensitive structs (`epoll_event`, `struct stat`, `sigaction`,
  `timespec`, `sockaddr_in`) must come from the RTL on FPC — never assume the
  x86-64 layout there. Delphi's Linux target is x86-64 only.

## Style
- All comments, identifiers and messages are in **English**.
- Pascal `{ }` comments do **not** nest: never put `{` or a `{$...}` directive
  inside a `{ }` comment — the first `}` ends it and the rest becomes code.
- Event masks are Pascal sets (`TEvEvents`); enum ordinals equal the C bit
  numbers. On Windows an "fd" is the `SOCKET` value itself.
- Keep the example programs simple and self-contained; do not add defensive
  complexity a demo does not need.

## Never
- Do **not** add any AI-attribution text anywhere — no `Co-Authored-By`, no
  "generated by/with", no assistant or tool signatures — in code, comments or
  commit messages.

## Before approving a change
- CI must stay green on all five targets (Linux x86-64 / arm64 / arm32,
  Windows, macOS). Every demo except the interactive `KeyDemo` is
  self-checking and exits non-zero on failure.
- Document new public API in `docs/MANUAL.md`, and in `README.md` if it is
  user-facing.
